mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month expansion in the industrial production index signals improving corporate revenue expectations and operating leverage, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month changes in INDPRO fail to achieve a Rank IC above 0.03 with 20-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, the restrictive cost of capital exceeds estimated nominal GDP growth, triggering equity risk premium expansion and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Mean 20-day S&P 500 forward returns during months with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% are not lower than the unconditional mean return over any 2-year rolling window (one-tailed t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in retail sales and residential construction starts indicates a broad demand recession that compresses aggregate earnings expectations more than either signal alone, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction-driven composite of RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce a Rank IC below -0.03 with statistical significance against 60-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, month-over-month declines in nonfarm payrolls signal exhaustion of labor market momentum that precedes earnings downgrades, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month declines in PAYEMS during late-cycle expansion periods do not predict negative 5-day S&P 500 returns with a success rate exceeding 55% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in retail sales signals stronger-than-expected consumer spending that drives positive earnings surprises and S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS month-over-month changes fail to achieve a Rank IC above 0.03 with 5-day forward returns over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth below trend alongside a federal funds rate above 3% confirms restrictive monetary policy is inducing a growth slowdown, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth < 1.5% and FEDFUNDS > 3.0%
failure_condition: The triggered GDPC1-FEDFUNDS signal fails to achieve a Rank IC below -0.03 with 60-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid 0.5 percentage point increase in the unemployment rate over a two-month window signals labor market deterioration that precedes consumption collapses and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE increases exceeding 0.5pp in 2 months fail to produce negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production expanding while consumer price growth decelerates indicates a positive supply shock that improves real margins and valuation multiples, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of INDPRO expansion and CPIAUCSL deceleration fails to achieve a Rank IC above 0.03 with 60-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.
